AE82043FDE130E503897B7315450B693: . When the user requests to disarm the area, the user is first checked to see if they have permission to disarm the area normally. If not, they are then checked to see if they have permission to temporarily disarm the area. This allows normal disarm permissions to take precedence. This occurs with any disarm request from the user, be it from a terminal, the web interface, card swipe or remote fob button press.
B6895245CBB4C62AE4B54C84E660B213: The remote hardware type should match the type of remote that this template will be linked with. The selected type will determine what options are available below. For Inovonics systems, all remotes are supported, however which actions are performed depend on the model of the remote.

510808CE53957C5BEF28DF8E1DD9491E: A user is any person that interacts with the Inception System by either badging a credential, entering a pin number or logging into the web interface. Every user will have one or more credentials and some permissions that determine how and what they can interact with in the system. This page is used to create or manage users, assign credentials and RF Fobs and assign permissions for the Inception Controller and web interface.
17ECA20AC4FA48B0D9F1EF58190702D0: columns are exported, only the first credential or fob number assigned to a user will be exported due to CSV format limitations. Similarly, if the
F35193B949BF20DC46ED6E73D1F6F9B7: This page is used to create and edit remote fob templates.
902CA349A55B4F80B748A37EBE7ABB4A: Remote fob templates are used when assigning remote fobs to users. The remote fob template contains the type of remote and describes what each button on the remote will do. When assigning a remote fob to a user, the remote fob template is selected and the serial number of the remote fob is typed in. This allows multiple users to be given remotes that have the same button behaviour and if any behaviour changes are required it can be edited in one place. Multiple remote fob templates can also be created if different users require different button behaviours.
08392DCFD886697835991DE1A1C01F2B: Without a correctly programmed remote fob template, the buttons on the remote fob will not do anything.

9C375B0B647449F5677D22B5F36B4DD6: This dialog allows you to assign a fob to a user based off of a recent (i.e. within the last 24 hours) unassigned fob event.
B3A266BB13BDF29C7B423B563E8C2935: Entries for the "Credentials" and "Remote Fobs" fields should contain only the Credential/Fob number; the Credential Template and Fob Template are specified below in the import options.
74BEAFEBA77621D34D627D5F0AFC83FA: Fields consisting of lists of items (e.g. Credentials, Remote Fob, Permissions) can only accept a single item at a time due to the limitations of the CSV format. For newly imported Users, the single item will become the only item in the list field. For existing users, the single item will be appended to the existing list of items (if it is not already present). In the case where you wish to import a User(s) with 2 credentials assigned to them, for example: you can CSV import the User(s) initially with a single credential number specified in the Credentials field, and then perform another CSV import with the same Users' names but with the 2nd credential number in the Credentials field, and the existing User will gain the 2nd credential number in their Credentials list.
